text:
## Summary
On 2026-02-20, one dedicated customer cluster experienced a routing loop between the edge router and the regional policy router after a policy update. This caused request amplification, elevated tail latency, and intermittent 503s for ~19 minutes until policy rollback.

## Severity
P0

## Customer impact
- Impacted: 1 dedicated customer environment in us-west (production).
- Symptoms: p99 latency increased from ~1.2s to > 18s; peak 503 rate 11%.

## Timeline (UTC)
- 05:14: Policy update applied (new fallback rule).
- 05:18: Latency alerts fire; on-call engaged.
- 05:25: Suspected loop; rollback initiated.
- 05:33: Metrics normalize.

## Root cause
A misconfigured fallback route allowed traffic to be re-ingested by the edge router with no loop detection. A recent refactor removed the hop-count header from dedicated routing.

## Detection
Detected via dedicated SLO alerting (...

title: Usage Insights Query Service (UIQS) — system and service doc
preview:
## Overview
The **Usage Insights Query Service (UIQS)** is the backend service that powers in-console cost/usage breakdowns by **API route** and **model** (and optional dimensions like workspace/project, region, and deployment mode). It provides a low-latency query API for interactive console tables and driver expla...
text:
## Overview
The **Usage Insights Query Service (UIQS)** is the backend service that powers in-console cost/usage breakdowns by **API route** and **model** (and optional dimensions like workspace/project, region, and deployment mode). It provides a low-latency query API for interactive console tables and driver explanations, while enforcing cost-visibility RBAC and protecting the telemetry warehouse from unbounded queries.

UIQS does **not** compute attribution itself. It queries pre-aggregated datasets produced by Telemetry & Analytics jobs and applies additional filtering, bucketing, pagination, and explainability rules.

### Primary consumers
- **Redwood Console** (Usage → Cost → Route/Model views)
- Internal tools for reconciliation/debugging (eng + support)
- Export job subsystem (async CSV generation; see API contract for lifecycle)

### Owners
- Product area: Console Insights (M...

text:
## Overview
This page specifies the Grafana dashboards used to monitor the production **perf-canary** service. The dashboards are designed for:
- Fast detection of per-model regressions in **p95/p99 latency** and **tokens/sec**
- Fast attribution to likely root cause area (kernel vs scheduler/queueing vs model config vs fleet)
- Direct navigation from an alert into dashboards, traces, and logs

These dashboards intentionally focus on **synthetic canary traffic** only (no customer prompts/data).

## Audience
- Serving Runtime (kernel/scheduling/instrumentation)
- SRE / on-call
- Telemetry / Observability platform
- Release Engineering (rollout gates)

## Dashboard set (Grafana)
We maintain three dashboards under the folder: **Runtime > Perf Canary**.

1) **Perf Canary / Overview**
 - Purpose: “Is anything broken right now?” and quick comparison across models/regions.

2) **Perf Canary ...

title: ADR-0143: Changelog generation source — registry/control-plane events vs Git tags/PR labels (vs hybrid)
preview:
## Status
Published (effective starting with Model Metadata Schema v1 rollout).

## Context
Redwood Inference is standardizing model/version metadata and generating customer-visible release notes for model/variant/quantization profile changes.

We need a reliable, auditable source for changelog generation that suppo...
text:
## Status
Published (effective starting with Model Metadata Schema v1 rollout).

## Context
Redwood Inference is standardizing model/version metadata and generating customer-visible release notes for model/variant/quantization profile changes.

We need a reliable, auditable source for changelog generation that supports:
- Customer-visible release notes (Console + docs site)
- Internal auditability (who changed what/when/where)
- Safe rollout policies (canary, A/B, promotion, rollback)
- Multi-deployment reality (Hosted vs Dedicated vs Private timing differences)

Historically, change visibility has been fragmented:
- Some change info lives in the model registry records
- Promotions happen through control plane workflows
- Human-authored release notes are sometimes captured via GitHub PR text/labels

This ADR decides the primary source-of-truth for changelog generation and the role of ...

text:
## Status
**Accepted (v1).** Implemented in the signal aggregation + routing service and used for HubSpot routing.

## Context
Redwood is implementing a privacy-aware pipeline that detects product-led signals (usage thresholds + buying intent), scores them, and routes qualified items into HubSpot for follow-up.

We need a **canonical, versioned signal object** that:
- Works for both **threshold** signals (e.g., spend velocity, sustained tokens, 429 rate) and **intent** signals (e.g., Dedicated/Private docs interest, security FAQ views).
- Supports **aggregation windows** (daily/weekly/30d), **deduping**, and **suppression** (to reduce spam).
- Allows deterministic **scoring** + routing, with auditability.
- Minimizes sensitive data, aligning with the PLG signal data handling policy.

## Decision
Adopt a single canonical record called **PLG Signal (schema v1)** emitted by the scoring s...

title: Onboarding Event Schema (v1) — Hosted API onboarding + checklist progress
preview:
## Overview
This page defines the **standardized event schema (v1)** for onboarding and checklist progress tracking across Redwood Inference (Hosted API first; Dedicated/Private later). The goal is consistent instrumentation from:
- **Gateway / serving edge** (server-side events for request outcomes)
- **Control pla...
text:
## Overview
This page defines the **standardized event schema (v1)** for onboarding and checklist progress tracking across Redwood Inference (Hosted API first; Dedicated/Private later). The goal is consistent instrumentation from:
- **Gateway / serving edge** (server-side events for request outcomes)
- **Control plane** (API key creation, quotas/budgets, model pinning, routing/fallback policy)
- **Console** (page views / actions that do not have a server-side equivalent)

This schema is used by:
- The **Checklist Progress Service** (step completion state)
- The **Onboarding KPI dashboards** (TTFS/TTFP, funnel, drop-offs)
- Support tooling (interpreting “what happened” without collecting sensitive payloads)

## Goals
1. Provide a single, validated envelope for onboarding events.
2. Ensure events are **PII-free** and do not include prompts/responses.
3. Support **idempotent** and **orde...

title: ADR-048: Experiment metrics attribution schema
preview:
## Status
Published.

## Summary
This ADR defines the canonical attribution dimensions and event schema used to measure route-level experiments (A/B/n) in Redwood Inference. The goals are:
- Ensure all experiment-related latency/cost/error/throughput metrics can be consistently attributed by {route, experiment_id, v...
text:
## Status
Published.

## Summary
This ADR defines the canonical attribution dimensions and event schema used to measure route-level experiments (A/B/n) in Redwood Inference. The goals are:
- Ensure all experiment-related latency/cost/error/throughput metrics can be consistently attributed by {route, experiment_id, variant}.
- Provide a stable contract between data plane (router/runtime), telemetry ingestion, analytics rollups, and Console.
- Avoid unbounded cardinality and cost blow-ups while keeping metrics useful for debugging and guardrails.

This ADR is intentionally minimal: it defines *required* dimensions and *required* per-request metrics, plus an optional token-level event intended primarily for debugging and TTFT/streaming investigations.

## Context
Route-level experiments are applied at the routing layer and may select different models, quantization profiles, or capacity t...
links: Audit logging: experiment config audit events

title: ADR-2025-02: Onboarding checklist eventing (server-side emission, client hints, schema versioning, progress computation)
preview:
## Summary
We are launching an in-product onboarding + “first production launch” checklist in the Redwood Console. This ADR documents how we instrument step completion and compute checklist progress.

**Decision (high level):**
- Emit **authoritative onboarding events server-side** (API gateway / control plane / bil...
text:
## Summary
We are launching an in-product onboarding + “first production launch” checklist in the Redwood Console. This ADR documents how we instrument step completion and compute checklist progress.

**Decision (high level):**
- Emit **authoritative onboarding events server-side** (API gateway / control plane / billing & quotas services) as the primary signal.
- Allow **limited client hints** from SDKs only where the server cannot reliably observe the user action (e.g., “user enabled streaming reconnect helper”). Client hints are advisory and do not override server signals.
- Use **schema versioning** with explicit `schema_version` and backward-compatible evolution. Breaking changes require a new major version.
- Compute progress using an **event-driven state machine** (monotonic step transitions) with a small number of **derived** completions for convenience (derived completions nev...
